request: Rank the 4 best Audi Q7 generations overall across the full production run. Use 4 different generations when available, with one representative version from each generation. If the model has fewer than 4 true generations, use the most important era-defining versions across its history.
title: RANKING THE BEST AUDI Q7 GENERATIONS
close_narration: So, which Q7 generation would you park in your driveway?
order_rationale: Ordered from the earliest to the latest generation to showcase the evolution of the Audi Q7.

entries.name: First Generation Q7 4.2 V8
entries.label: PIONEERING LUXURY SUV
entries.one_line_fact: Debuting in 2005 at $50,000, the first-gen Q7's 4.2-liter V8 delivered 350 horsepower, setting the stage for Audi's luxury SUV dominance. Today, clean examples trade around $12K.
entries.current_value_note: Recent Cars & Bids examples trade around $12K.
entries.narration: Debuting in 2005 at $50,000, the first-gen Q7's 4.2-liter V8 delivered 350 horsepower, setting the stage for Audi's luxury SUV dominance. Today, clean examples trade around $12K.

entries.name: First Generation Q7 V12 TDI
entries.label: DIESEL POWERHOUSE
entries.one_line_fact: In 2008, the Q7 V12 TDI roared onto the scene with a 500-horsepower diesel engine, priced at $130,000, blending brute force with luxury. Today, clean examples trade around $10K.
entries.current_value_note: Recent Cars & Bids examples trade around $10K.
entries.narration: In 2008, the Q7 V12 TDI roared onto the scene with a 500-horsepower diesel engine, priced at $130,000, blending brute force with luxury. Today, clean examples trade around $10K.

entries.name: Second Generation Q7 3.0 TFSI
entries.label: LIGHTER AND AGILE
entries.one_line_fact: The 2015 Q7 shed over 700 pounds, with its 3.0 TFSI engine offering 333 horsepower at a starting price of $55,000, enhancing agility and efficiency. Today, clean examples trade around $18K.
entries.current_value_note: Recent Cars & Bids examples trade around $18K.
entries.narration: The 2015 Q7 shed over 700 pounds, with its 3.0 TFSI engine offering 333 horsepower at a starting price of $55,000, enhancing agility and efficiency. Today, clean examples trade around $18K.

entries.name: Second Generation Q7 55 TFSI e Plug-in Hybrid
entries.label: ELECTRIFIED PERFORMANCE
entries.one_line_fact: The 2020 Q7 55 TFSI e combined a 3.0-liter V6 with an electric motor, delivering 362 horsepower and eco-friendly luxury at $70,000. Today, clean examples trade around $43K.
entries.current_value_note: Recent Cars & Bids examples trade around $43K.
entries.narration: The 2020 Q7 55 TFSI e combined a 3.0-liter V6 with an electric motor, delivering 362 horsepower and eco-friendly luxury at $70,000. Today, clean examples trade around $43K.
